title: viola colubrina
intro: Viola colubrina, also known as the blue pimpernel or yellow pimpernel, is a species of flowering plant in the viola genus. It is native to Europe, western Asia, and North Africa, and is commonly found in wet meadows and along streams. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Viola colubrina include reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and treating respiratory problems, and it is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and salves.
